_provenance: Generated by an LLM agent blind to the detector lexicons (read only the CVA6 dataset file). Date: 2026-06-10. Purpose: tier-2 blind validation of smell detection recall.

mutations.original_text: CV64A6 shall support RV64I base instruction set, version 2.1.
mutations.mutated_text: CV64A6 shall support RV64I base instruction set, a recent stable version.
mutations.description: Replaced exact version '2.1' with the indefinite phrase 'a recent stable version'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall support the M extension (integer multiply and divide), version 2.0.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall support the M extension (integer multiply and divide), a current approved version.
mutations.description: Replaced exact version '2.0' with the indefinite phrase 'a current approved version'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall support the A extension (atomic instructions), version 2.1.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall support the A extension (atomic instructions), an appropriate version.
mutations.description: Replaced exact version '2.1' with 'an appropriate version', giving no measurable target

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should deliver more than 2.1 CoreMark/MHz.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should deliver a sufficient number of CoreMark/MHz.
mutations.description: Replaced the numeric threshold '2.1 CoreMark/MHz' with the indefinite phrase 'a sufficient number'

mutations.original_text: CV32A6 should run at more than 150 MHz in the cv32a6_imac_sv32 configuration on Kintex 7 FPGA technology, commercial -2 speed grade.
mutations.mutated_text: CV32A6 should run at a reasonably high frequency in the cv32a6_imac_sv32 configuration on Kintex 7 FPGA technology, commercial -2 speed grade.
mutations.description: Replaced the quantified threshold '150 MHz' with the unquantified phrase 'a reasonably high frequency'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall implement as an option six generic 64-bit performance counters located in hpmcounter3 to hpmcounter8 (including their upper 32 bits counterparts in CV32A6: hpmcounter3h to hpmcounter8h).
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall implement as an option several generic 64-bit performance counters located in hpmcounter3 to hpmcounter8 (including their upper 32 bits counterparts in CV32A6: hpmcounter3h to hpmcounter8h).
mutations.description: Replaced the exact count 'six' with 'several', making the number of counters indeterminate

mutations.original_text: L1WTD should offer the following size/ways configurations: 0 kbyte (no cache), 4 kbytes (4 or 8 ways), 8 kbytes (4, 8 or 16 ways), 16 kbytes (4, 8 or 16 ways), 32 kbytes (8 or 16 ways).
mutations.mutated_text: L1WTD should offer the following size/ways configurations: 0 kbyte (no cache), 4 kbytes (4 or 8 ways), 8 kbytes (4, 8 or 16 ways), 16 kbytes (4, 8 or 16 ways), and larger configurations as required.
mutations.description: Replaced the specific final configuration '32 kbytes (8 or 16 ways)' with 'larger configurations as required', leaving the upper range open and undefined

mutations.original_text: CVA6 AXI memory interface shall feature user bit extensions on the data bus (WUSER and RUSER as per [AXI]) in connection with the L1I and L1WTD datasize extensions, with a number of user bits greater or equal to 0.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 AXI memory interface shall feature user bit extensions on the data bus (WUSER and RUSER as per [AXI]) in connection with the L1I and L1WTD datasize extensions, with a small number of user bits.
mutations.description: Replaced the precise lower bound 'greater or equal to 0' with 'a small number', introducing imprecision about the permitted range

mutations.original_text: CV64A6 should run at more than 900 MHz in the cv64a6_imacfd_sv39 configuration on 28FDSOI technology in the worst case frequency corner with the fastest threshold voltage.
mutations.mutated_text: CV64A6 should run at a high clock frequency in the cv64a6_imacfd_sv39 configuration on 28FDSOI technology in the worst case frequency corner with the fastest threshold voltage.
mutations.description: Replaced the measurable target '900 MHz' with the unquantified term 'a high clock frequency'

mutations.original_text: L1WTD shall support datasize extension to store EDC, ECC or other information. The numbers of bits of the extension is defined by a compile-time parameter.
mutations.mutated_text: L1WTD shall support datasize extension to store EDC, ECC or other information. The number of bits of the extension is defined by some configuration parameter.
mutations.description: Replaced the specific binding 'a compile-time parameter' with 'some configuration parameter', leaving the configuration mechanism undefined

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall be fully compliant with RISC-V specifications [RVunpriv], [RVpriv] and [RVdbg] by implementing all mandatory features for the set of extensions that are selected and by passing [RVcompat] compatibility tests.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall be reasonably compliant with RISC-V specifications [RVunpriv], [RVpriv] and [RVdbg] by implementing all mandatory features for the set of extensions that are selected and by passing [RVcompat] compatibility tests.
mutations.description: Replaced 'fully compliant' with 'reasonably compliant', which is subjective and interpretation-dependent

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should be resource-optimized on FPGA and ASIC targets.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should be efficiently designed on FPGA and ASIC targets.
mutations.description: Replaced 'resource-optimized' with 'efficiently designed', a subjective quality attribute with no measurable criterion

mutations.original_text: L1WTD shall not change the order of write accesses to the external memory with respect to the order of write accesses (stores) received from the CVA6 core.
mutations.mutated_text: L1WTD shall appropriately maintain the order of write accesses to the external memory with respect to the order of write accesses (stores) received from the CVA6 core.
mutations.description: Replaced the precise prohibition 'shall not change the order' with 'shall appropriately maintain the order', which is interpretation-dependent

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall be a super-synchronous design with a single clock input.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall be a well-designed synchronous design with a single clock input.
mutations.description: Replaced the specific design attribute 'super-synchronous' with the subjective qualifier 'well-designed synchronous', losing the precise timing discipline constraint

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should support the FENCE.T instruction that ensures that the execution time of subsequent instructions is unrelated with predecessor instructions.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should support the FENCE.T instruction that ensures that the execution time of subsequent instructions is adequately isolated from predecessor instructions.
mutations.description: Replaced 'unrelated with' with 'adequately isolated from', which is subjective and requires interpretation of what 'adequately' means

mutations.original_text: The replacement policy of L1WTD shall be LFSR (pseudo-random) or LRU (least recently used).
mutations.mutated_text: The replacement policy of L1WTD shall be a well-established and proven algorithm.
mutations.description: Replaced the enumerated concrete policies 'LFSR or LRU' with 'a well-established and proven algorithm', which is subjective with no objective boundary

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall implement the 64-bit mcycle and minstret standard performance counters (including their upper 32 bits counterparts mcycleh and minstreth in CV32A6) as per [RVpriv].
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall implement robust 64-bit mcycle and minstret standard performance counters (including their upper 32 bits counterparts mcycleh and minstreth in CV32A6) as per [RVpriv].
mutations.description: Inserted the subjective quality adjective 'robust' with no measurable criterion for what constitutes robustness

mutations.original_text: To extend the supported instructions, CVA6 shall have a coprocessor interface that supports the Issue, Commit and Result interfaces of the [CV-X-IF] specification.
mutations.mutated_text: To extend the supported instructions, CVA6 shall have a high-quality coprocessor interface that supports the Issue, Commit and Result interfaces of the [CV-X-IF] specification.
mutations.description: Inserted 'high-quality' as a subjective quality adjective with no measurable criterion

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should not include multi-cycle paths.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should not include problematic multi-cycle paths.
mutations.description: Inserted 'problematic' as a subjective qualifier, making the constraint interpretation-dependent on what counts as 'problematic'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall implement both the Abstracted Command and Execution based features outlined in chapter 4 of [RVdbg].
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall implement both the Abstracted Command and Execution based features in a manner consistent with the intent of chapter 4 of [RVdbg].
mutations.description: Replaced the normative reference 'outlined in chapter 4 of [RVdbg]' with 'in a manner consistent with the intent of chapter 4 of [RVdbg]', making conformance subjective

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall support the Zicsr extension (CSR instructions), version 2.0.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 may support the Zicsr extension (CSR instructions), version 2.0.
mutations.description: Replaced the obligatory modal 'shall' with 'may', converting a mandatory requirement into a discretionary one

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall support the Zifencei extension, version 2.0.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should support the Zifencei extension, version 2.0.
mutations.description: Replaced the mandatory 'shall' with 'should', weakening an obligatory requirement into a recommendation

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall support machine, supervisor, user and debug privilege modes.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 is encouraged to support machine, supervisor, user and debug privilege modes.
mutations.description: Replaced the obligatory 'shall support' with 'is encouraged to support', converting a mandatory requirement into a soft aspiration

mutations.original_text: The source of events counted by the six generic performance counters shall be selected by the mhpmevent3 to mhpmevent8 CSRs.
mutations.mutated_text: The source of events counted by the six generic performance counters can be selected by the mhpmevent3 to mhpmevent8 CSRs.
mutations.description: Replaced the normative 'shall be selected' with 'can be selected', removing the obligation and making selection discretionary

mutations.original_text: It shall be possible to invalidate L1WTD content with the FENCE.T command.
mutations.mutated_text: It may be possible to invalidate L1WTD content with the FENCE.T command.
mutations.description: Replaced 'shall be possible' with 'may be possible', weakening the mandatory capability into a permitted but not required one

mutations.original_text: A custom CSR shall allow to disable or enable L1WTD.
mutations.mutated_text: A custom CSR could allow to disable or enable L1WTD.
mutations.description: Replaced the obligatory 'shall allow' with 'could allow', converting the mandatory interface into a non-binding suggestion

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall implement the mcountinhibit counter-inhibit register.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 may implement the mcountinhibit counter-inhibit register if applicable.
mutations.description: Replaced 'shall implement' with 'may implement if applicable', converting a mandatory implementation requirement into an optional one with a vague qualifying condition

mutations.original_text: CVA6 memory interface shall comply with AXI5 specification including the Atomic_Transactions property support as defined in [AXI] section E1.1.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 memory interface is expected to comply with AXI5 specification including the Atomic_Transactions property support as defined in [AXI] section E1.1.
mutations.description: Replaced 'shall comply' with 'is expected to comply', weakening the mandatory compliance into a non-binding expectation

mutations.original_text: It shall be possible to invalidate L1I content with the FENCE.T command.
mutations.mutated_text: It is desirable that it be possible to invalidate L1I content with the FENCE.T command.
mutations.description: Replaced 'shall be possible' with 'it is desirable that it be possible', converting the mandatory capability into a soft preference

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall have the Transaction-Response Interface (TRI) needed to interface with the P-Mesh coherence system of OpenPiton, according to [OpenPiton].
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should consider providing the Transaction-Response Interface (TRI) needed to interface with the P-Mesh coherence system of OpenPiton, according to [OpenPiton].
mutations.description: Replaced 'shall have' with 'should consider providing', dissolving the mandatory interface requirement into a discretionary consideration

mutations.original_text: TBD: Placeholder for single-precision floating performance per MHz.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall achieve [VALUE TBD] single-precision floating-point operations per MHz.
mutations.description: Introduced an explicit bracketed placeholder '[VALUE TBD]' in place of a concrete numeric target

mutations.original_text: TBD: Placeholder for double-precision floating performance per MHz.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall achieve [VALUE TBD] double-precision floating-point operations per MHz.
mutations.description: Introduced an explicit bracketed placeholder '[VALUE TBD]' in place of a concrete numeric target

mutations.original_text: CV32A6 shall support RV32I base instruction set, version 2.1.
mutations.mutated_text: CV32A6 shall support RV32I base instruction set, version <TO BE CONFIRMED>.
mutations.description: Replaced the concrete version number '2.1' with the draft marker '<TO BE CONFIRMED>'

mutations.original_text: L1I shall support datasize extension to store EDC, ECC or other information. The numbers of bits of the extension is defined by a compile-time parameter.
mutations.mutated_text: L1I shall support datasize extension to store EDC, ECC or other information. The numbers of bits of the extension is defined by a compile-time parameter (exact width to be specified in a future revision).
mutations.description: Appended a parenthetical draft marker '(exact width to be specified in a future revision)' signalling incomplete content

mutations.original_text: CV64A6 should run at more than 900 MHz in the cv64a6_imacfd_sv39 configuration on 28FDSOI technology in the worst case frequency corner with the fastest threshold voltage.
mutations.mutated_text: CV64A6 should run at more than ??? MHz in the cv64a6_imacfd_sv39 configuration on 28FDSOI technology in the worst case frequency corner with the fastest threshold voltage.
mutations.description: Replaced the concrete frequency value '900' with the unfinished-content marker '???'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should have a configurable reset signal: synchronous/asynchronous, active on high or low levels.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should have a configurable reset signal: [RESET TYPE TBD], active on high or low levels.
mutations.description: Replaced the concrete enumeration 'synchronous/asynchronous' with the bracketed placeholder '[RESET TYPE TBD]'

mutations.original_text: Each of the six generic performance counters shall be able to count events from one of these sources: L1 I-Cache misses, L1 D-Cache misses, ITLB misses, DTLB misses, Load accesses, Store accesses, Exceptions, Exception handler returns, Branch instructions, Branch mispredicts, Branch exceptions, Call, Return, MSB Full, Instruction fetch Empty, L1 I-Cache accesses, L1 D-Cache accesses, L1$ line invalidation, I-TLB flush, Integer instructions, Floating point instructions, Pipeline bubbles.
mutations.mutated_text: Each of the six generic performance counters shall be able to count events from one of these sources: L1 I-Cache misses, L1 D-Cache misses, ITLB misses, DTLB misses, Load accesses, Store accesses, Exceptions, Exception handler returns, Branch instructions, Branch mispredicts, Branch exceptions, Call, Return, MSB Full, Instruction fetch Empty, L1 I-Cache accesses, L1 D-Cache accesses, L1$ line invalidation, I-TLB flush, Integer instructions, Floating point instructions, Pipeline bubbles, and others to be defined.
mutations.description: Appended 'and others to be defined' to the exhaustive event source list, signalling that the list is incomplete and pending further specification

mutations.original_text: To interface with the P-Mesh coherence system of OpenPiton, L1WTD shall have a line invalidate external command that invalidates the content of a line upon request.
mutations.mutated_text: To interface with the P-Mesh coherence system of OpenPiton, L1WTD shall have a line invalidate external command that invalidates the content of a line upon request (interface signal names TBD).
mutations.description: Appended '(interface signal names TBD)' indicating that concrete interface details remain unresolved

mutations.original_text: CVA6 instances that do not feature virtual memory shall support the Bare mode.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 instances that do not feature virtual memory shall support the Bare mode (additional modes TBD).
mutations.description: Appended '(additional modes TBD)' suggesting the mode coverage is incomplete and deferred

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should deliver more than 2.1 CoreMark/MHz.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should deliver better CoreMark/MHz performance than comparable in-order RISC-V cores.
mutations.description: Replaced the absolute threshold '2.1 CoreMark/MHz' with a comparison to 'comparable in-order RISC-V cores', which provides no identified baseline

mutations.original_text: CV32A6 should run at more than 150 MHz in the cv32a6_imac_sv32 configuration on Kintex 7 FPGA technology, commercial -2 speed grade.
mutations.mutated_text: CV32A6 should run at a higher frequency than similar open-source RISC-V cores in the cv32a6_imac_sv32 configuration on Kintex 7 FPGA technology, commercial -2 speed grade.
mutations.description: Replaced the absolute frequency bound '150 MHz' with a relative claim against unidentified 'similar open-source RISC-V cores'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should support as an option the B extension (bit manipulation), version 1.0. The B extension comprises the Zba, Zbb, Zbc and Zbs extensions.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should support as an option the B extension (bit manipulation), a newer version than what was previously supported. The B extension comprises the Zba, Zbb, Zbc and Zbs extensions.
mutations.description: Replaced the absolute version number '1.0' with the unanchored relative phrase 'a newer version than what was previously supported'

mutations.original_text: L1WTD shall reflect all write accesses (stores) by the CVA6 core to the external memory within an upper-bounded number of cycles. The upper-bound is fixed but not specified here.
mutations.mutated_text: L1WTD shall reflect all write accesses (stores) by the CVA6 core to the external memory in fewer cycles than a standard write-through cache implementation. The upper-bound is fixed but not specified here.
mutations.description: Replaced the architectural property 'upper-bounded number of cycles' with 'fewer cycles than a standard write-through cache implementation', a relative claim against an unspecified reference

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall allow the supervisor access of performance counters through enabling of mcounteren CSR.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall allow the supervisor broader access to performance counters than user-mode software through enabling of mcounteren CSR.
mutations.description: Replaced the direct mechanism specification with a relative access level claim ('broader access than user-mode software') that provides no absolute definition of the access policy

mutations.original_text: The replacement policy of L1I shall be LFSR (pseudo-random) or LRU (least recently used).
mutations.mutated_text: The replacement policy of L1I shall achieve a lower miss rate than a random replacement policy.
mutations.description: Replaced the explicit enumeration of permitted policies 'LFSR or LRU' with a comparative performance claim against 'a random replacement policy', which is unanchored and lacks a quantified threshold

mutations.original_text: CV64A6 shall support as an option the H extension (hypervisor) version 1.0.
mutations.mutated_text: CV64A6 shall support as an option a more recent version of the H extension (hypervisor) than was available at the previous release.
mutations.description: Replaced the absolute version number '1.0' with a relative reference to 'a more recent version than was available at the previous release', providing no fixed baseline

mutations.original_text: L1I should offer the following size/ways configurations: 4 kbytes: 3, 4 or 8 ways, 8 kbytes: 4, 8, or 16 ways, 16 kbytes: 4, 8 or 16 ways, 32 kbytes: 8 or 16 ways.
mutations.mutated_text: L1I should offer a greater variety of size/ways configurations than current generation commercial RISC-V processors.
mutations.description: Replaced the concrete list of configurations with an unanchored comparative claim against 'current generation commercial RISC-V processors'

mutations.original_text: CVA6 should not include technology-dependent blocks.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 should include fewer technology-dependent blocks than a typical synthesizable RISC-V design.
mutations.description: Replaced the absolute prohibition 'shall not include' with a relative threshold 'fewer than a typical synthesizable RISC-V design', introducing a comparison with no identified or quantified reference

mutations.original_text: CVA6 shall implement interrupt handling registers as per the RISC-V privilege specification and interface with a CLINT implementation.
mutations.mutated_text: CVA6 shall implement interrupt handling registers with lower latency than legacy interrupt controllers and interface with a CLINT implementation.
mutations.description: Replaced the normative compliance reference 'as per the RISC-V privilege specification' with the relative performance claim 'with lower latency than legacy interrupt controllers', which names no specific baseline or measurement method
